title: Elizabeth Stepp
entries: She is great at explaining concepts. She will take time to make sure students questions are worked out. Sends an email at the beginning of the week clarifying what's due by the end of the week

She is super kind and funny! She explains things well and I like her lecture style (writing down notes and examples). Tests are straightforward. She will go over things several times if you don't understand. Overall great professor and super nice.
She is a great professor but is a very harsh grader and assumes you know the material already.
Super nice teacher. Haven't finished the class yet, but she explains all the material very well so far. No complaints!

she was okay. attendance didn't matter and the lectures were online and recorded. felt bad because not a lot of people responded during zooms, but also the class was boring and a bit disorganized. Felt like lectures didn't help too much, it was mainly my TA and practice problems that helped me on exams. she did not know much about the exams at all
No curve at all throughout the semester and extremely difficult final. She's kind of mediocre, a bit disorganized. Lots of practices and self study needed to get a good grade.

She is very disorganized and doesn't know much about the tests being given. Attendence isn't needed but if you've never taken calc this class will be kinda hard. I just took it for my calc requirement for the bio major and struggled a lot. No curve was given either
maybe the most unorganized person ever. do not take her if you are already not an A+ student in math. run

It would be difficult to take this class if you've never taken calc before, because the lectures were taught in a way that felt as though you should already know key calculus concepts. Even though I took calculus in high school, I found the course challenging and relied on lots of information I learned in high school.
She was my 8 am, slept through most of her (online) classes, still passed.

lectures and notes could be confusing, but she posted recordings and digital notes which helped. problems on homework and exams were harder than those reviewed in class, but the final was not as hard as the exams before it. there were weekly quizzes, but some were dropped

Very tough class. I had to get tutoring throughtout the entire semester. I had taken AP calc which prepared me a little, but if you haven't taken calc before then I would not take. Her teaching style is quite confusing and there is little instruction. The only things that saved my grade were the curve for the final and all the online homework.
Professor Stepp is an amazing Professor. She cares greatly for her students, and her online lectures are awesome. Don't hesitate to take her class.

Prof. Stepp is a great person, but I don't think she is the best at teaching. She assumes that most people already have knowledge about probability/statistics, which sucks if you don't. Her notes are a bit of a mess but they are not too bad. 1 midterm and 1 final, no curve. She's hilarious though, and shows us her cats all the time.

Do not take her. Especially if you are taking another stem course alongside. everyone does badly on the midterms and there is no curve. lectures are based on her knowledge not the book. Had to self-teach myself and at the end i wasn't willing to get a C on my transcript so i withdrew. Asked her for help and she was not helpful and even rude.

kind of the goat tbh, super nice and explains concepts clearly w lots of examples. i personally didnt find her notes confusing and she was pretty okay about recording lectures. exams were way harder than the material taught in the lectures tho, mid 70s avgs on both midterms so far, no EC. long hws and webassigns but shes still a great prof imo
prof stepp's notes are disorganized and her explanations are always unclear and difficult to comprehend
i gotta run, but, to whom it may concern: do not tak

Made the online class interactive and encouraged participation. Will definitely be on her good side if you go to all the lectures and make an effort to participate. Go to class because notes can be confusing at times and lecture recordings are not guaranteed! Weekly hws but they prepared you for the tests + quizzes so definitely do them.

Professor Stepp was decent at teaching concepts, but her notes could be confusing on times. I mostly just focused on doing the homework and it was similar to the tests. For the midterms, the first one was difficult, but the final and the 2nd midterm were not too bad. She is really helpful if you ask her questions and the quizzes were really easy.
